documentation.readme: Features • Installation • Usage • Running tlsx • Join Discord
usage_sections: Features: - Fast And fully configurable TLS Connection - Multiple Modes for TLS Connection - Multiple TLS probes - Auto TLS Fallback for older TLS version - Pre Handshake TLS connection (early termination) - Customizable Cipher / SNI / TLS selection - JARM/JA3 TLS Fingerprint - TLS Misconf
Installation: tlsx requires Go 1.24 to install successfully. To install, just run the below command or download pre-compiled binary from release page.

arguments.name: -cert
arguments.description: include certificates in json output (PEM format)

arguments.name: -certificate
arguments.description: include certificates in json output (PEM format)

arguments.name: -ch
arguments.description: include client hello in json output (ztls mode only)

arguments.name: -client-hello
arguments.description: include client hello in json output (ztls mode only)

arguments.name: -dashboard
arguments.description: upload or view output in the PDCP UI dashboard

arguments.name: -dns
arguments.description: display unique hostname from SSL certificate response

arguments.name: -ex
arguments.description: display host with host expired certificate

arguments.name: -expired
arguments.description: display host with host expired certificate

arguments.name: -hardfail
arguments.description: strategy to use if encountered errors while checking revocation status

arguments.name: -hf
arguments.description: strategy to use if encountered errors while checking revocation status

arguments.name: -MODE
arguments.description: -sm, -scan-mode string tls connection mode to use (ctls, ztls, openssl, auto) (default "auto")

arguments.name: -pd
arguments.description: upload or view output in the PDCP UI dashboard

arguments.name: -pre-handshake
arguments.description: enable pre-handshake tls connection (early termination) using ztls

arguments.name: -ps
arguments.description: enable pre-handshake tls connection (early termination) using ztls

arguments.name: -rev-ptr-sni
arguments.description: perform reverse PTR to retrieve SNI from IP

arguments.name: -rps
arguments.description: perform reverse PTR to retrieve SNI from IP

arguments.name: -sa
arguments.description: scan all ips for a host (default false)

arguments.name: -scan-all-ips
arguments.description: scan all ips for a host (default false)

arguments.name: -server-hello
arguments.description: include server hello in json output (ztls mode only)

arguments.name: -sh
arguments.description: include server hello in json output (ztls mode only)

arguments.name: -tc
arguments.description: include certificates chain in json output

arguments.name: -tls-chain
arguments.description: include certificates chain in json output

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with projectdiscovery/uncover (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with projectdiscovery/subfinder (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with projectdiscovery/nuclei (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the recon category. Fits when the engagement needs: Features • Installation • Usage • Running tlsx • Join Discord.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Features • Installation • Usage • Running tlsx • Join Discord.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: go install github.com/projectdiscovery/tlsx/cmd/tlsx@latest. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `go install github.com/projectdiscovery/tlsx/cmd/tlsx@latest`. Further invocations from upstream: `tlsx -h`; `$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST/24 # CIDR input`. Features • Installation • Usage • Running tlsx • Join Discord.

name: tlsx
summary: Features • Installation • Usage • Running tlsx • Join Discord

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need recon, use tlsx because Features • Installation • Usage • Running tlsx • Join Discord.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `go install github.com/projectdiscovery/tlsx/cmd/tlsx@latest` as documented upstream for tlsx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tlsx -h` as documented upstream for tlsx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST/24 # CIDR input` as documented upstream for tlsx.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `$KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST # IP input` as documented upstream for tlsx.
When chaining recon tools, pair tlsx with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tlsx --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
